sections.paragraph:
- Original: Νάρκισσος

- Transliteration: Narkissos

- Phonetic: nar'-kis-sos

- Definition: Narcissus = "stupidity"

1. a dweller at Rome mentioned by Paul in Rom. 16:11

- Origin: "a flower of the same name, from narke (stupefaction, as a "narcotic")"

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Masculine

- Strong's: A flower of the same name from νάρκη narkē (stupefaction as a narcotic); Narcissus a Roman: - Narcissus.
